ret_greeting.ai_message: 3 retention signals surfaced overnight. Want me to walk through them?

ret_signal_1_churn_surge.ai_message: First: 312 members crossed the 70% churn probability threshold this week — up 58% from 198 last week. Einstein's model attributes the surge to a combination of rate dissatisfaction (CD and auto loan) and declining digital engagement. The average churn probability for this cohort is 76%. Total lifetime value at risk: $11.9M.

ret_signal_2_engagement_decay.ai_message: Second: digital engagement continues to decay — 1,243 members have not logged into any digital channel in 90+ days. This cohort grew 14% in the last month alone. 62% are single-product holders, which correlates with 2x higher churn risk compared to multi-product members. The total LTV at risk for this group is $34.1M.

ret_signal_3_retiree_disengage.ai_message: Third: the military retiree segment NPS dropped 13 points — from 52 to 39 — over the last 6 months. This is your highest-LTV segment with an average lifetime value of $172,000. Top detractor themes from Genesys transcripts: 'CD rate not competitive' (36%) and 'no loyalty recognition' (29%). This segment holds $31.6M in CD balances at risk of moving to Navy Federal or USAA.

ret_turn_1_churn_signals.ai_message:
I've pulled data from Einstein, Data Cloud, Snowflake, and Genesys to give you the full churn picture.

Over the last 12 weeks, the number of high-risk members (>70% churn probability) has grown from 142 to 312 — a 120% increase. The acceleration started in Week 8, coinciding with the CD rate changes.

[Churn Risk by Segment]
[1] Military Retirees: 214 members, 71% avg churn risk, $36.8M LTV at risk
[2] Single-Product Holders: 487 members, 62% avg churn risk, $18.2M LTV at risk
[3] First-Time Applicants (Stalled): 312 members, 64% avg churn risk, $11.9M LTV at risk
[4] Digital Dormant: 1,243 members, 58% avg churn risk, $34.1M LTV at risk
[5] Young Professionals (<30): 198 members, 55% avg churn risk, $6.3M LTV at risk

Total LTV at risk across all segments: $82.8M. The military retiree segment has the highest per-member LTV by far ($172K avg).
suggested_chips: Which segments are most at risk?

ret_turn_2_risk_segments.ai_message:
Here's the predictive risk scoring across all retention segments:

[1] Military Retirees — 71% avg churn risk | 214 members | $36.8M LTV
Driver: CD rate dissatisfaction + loyalty recognition gap
Projection: Without intervention, 68% likely to close CD within 90 days

[2] First-Time Applicants (Stalled) — 64% avg churn risk | 312 members | $11.9M LTV
Driver: Application friction + no follow-up
Projection: 52% likely to abandon USSFCU entirely within 60 days

[3] Single-Product Holders — 62% avg churn risk | 487 members | $18.2M LTV

[4] Digital Dormant — 58% avg churn risk | 1,243 members | $34.1M LTV
Driver: No digital activity, declining to branch-only
Projection: 38% likely to close within 180 days

Einstein models show that targeted outreach historically improves retention by 15.2 percentage points vs. no action (3.1% natural retention lift). The highest-impact intervention would be personalized rate offers for military retirees — historical data shows 40% reactivation rate.

actions.title: Generate retention brief for 312 high-churn members
actions.description: Create a one-page retention brief summarizing the 312 members who crossed the 70% threshold, their segments, drivers, and recommended interventions. Push to leadership Slack channel.
actions.impact: Enable leadership to prioritize retention budget allocation

actions.title: Build targeted outreach list — personalized rate offers for military retirees
actions.description: Create AgentForce segment of 214 military retirees with NPS decline >10 pts and CD balance >$50K. Attach personalized rate match offer based on competitive analysis.
actions.impact: Historical 40% reactivation rate for personalized offers — potential to retain $14.7M in CD balances

actions.title: Schedule re-engagement campaign for digital-dormant members
actions.description: Launch a 3-touch re-engagement sequence (SMS → Email → Phone) for 1,243 digital-dormant members, prioritized by LTV. Include exclusive rate offer and account review invitation.
actions.impact: Expected 18–30% reactivation based on channel mix (SMS: 18%, Phone: 30%)

ret_churn_drivers.ai_message:
Here are the top disengagement drivers this month, ranked by impact:

[1] CD Rate Dissatisfaction — affecting 214 military retirees + 89 other CD holders. Genesys transcripts show 'rate' mentioned in 68% of disengagement-correlated calls. USSFCU's 12-month CD rate (4.75%) is 25 bps below Navy Federal (5.00%) and 15 bps below USAA (4.90%).

[2] Application Friction — 312 first-time applicants stalled at mortgage document upload. 67% are mobile users who experienced the upload timeout. No follow-up outreach was sent.

[3] Low Product Penetration — 487 single-product holders showing declining engagement. Members with 1 product have 2.1x higher churn risk than those with 3+ products.

[4] Digital Channel Neglect — 1,243 members went dormant. 78% of these members last interacted via branch. Digital re-engagement touchpoints were insufficient.

[5] Loyalty Recognition Gap — Military retirees with 15+ year tenure feel their loyalty isn't rewarded. 29% of detractor transcripts mention 'loyalty' or 'tenure'.
suggested_chips: Which segments are most at risk?
Model the retention impact of outreach

Digital engagement decay over the last 6 months:

[90+ Day Dormant Members]
— Oct 2025: 824
— Nov 2025: 891 (+8%)
— Dec 2025: 942 (+6%)
— Jan 2026: 1,018 (+8%)
— Feb 2026: 1,089 (+7%)

The March acceleration correlates with reduced in-app engagement campaigns after the Q1 marketing budget reallocation. Meanwhile, active digital users declined from 14,200 to 13,310 — a 6.3% drop.

[Dormancy by Product Count]
— 1 product: 62% of dormant members (highest churn risk: 0.63)
— 2 products: 24% of dormant (churn risk: 0.51)

Single-product holders are clearly the most vulnerable. Cross-sell could reduce their churn risk by 22%.

ret_retiree_sentiment.ai_message:
Military retiree sentiment deep dive from Genesys transcript analysis:

[Detractor Themes — 214 members, NPS 39]
[1] 'CD rate not competitive' — 78 interactions (36%)
Members comparing USSFCU's 4.75% 12-month CD against Navy Federal (5.00%) and USAA (4.90%). Several mentioned moving $100K+ in balances.

[2] 'No loyalty recognition' — 62 interactions (29%)
Members with 15+ years feel their tenure doesn't earn better rates. Quote: 'I've been here 22 years and a new member gets the same rate as me.'

[3] 'Poor maturity communication' — 44 interactions (21%)
Members say they weren't notified before CD auto-renewed at a lower rate.

[4] 'Want to speak with manager' — 30 interactions (14%)
Escalation requests are 3x higher than other segments.

[Sentiment Score: 2.1 out of 5 (very negative)]

This segment has the highest individual LTV ($172K avg) and holds $31.6M in CD balances. A 25 bps rate match could retain 85–90% of at-risk members based on Einstein's model.

ret_impact_model.ai_message:
Retention impact modeling — with vs. without targeted outreach:

[Scenario A: No Action (Baseline)]
— Projected churn in 90 days: 487 members (27.5% of at-risk)
— LTV lost: $22.8M
— Natural retention lift: 3.1%

[Scenario B: Targeted Outreach (Recommended)]
— Projected churn in 90 days: 198 members (11.2% of at-risk)
— LTV preserved: $16.4M (vs. scenario A)
— Retention lift: 15.2% (historical average)
— Cost of outreach: ~$240K
— ROI: 68:1 (LTV preserved vs. outreach cost)

[Scenario C: Personalized Rate Match + Outreach]
— Projected churn in 90 days: 124 members (7.0% of at-risk)
— LTV preserved: $19.8M (vs. scenario A)
— Retention lift: 20.5%
— Cost: ~$580K (includes rate concessions)
— ROI: 34:1

Scenario B offers the best ROI. Scenario C is recommended specifically for the military retiree segment where the per-member LTV justifies the rate concession.

ret_retention_brief.ai_message:
Here's your retention brief for leadership:

[RETENTION ALERT — Week of April 1–7, 2026]

[Executive Summary]
1,769 members are at elevated churn risk, representing $82.8M in lifetime value. Three signals require immediate attention: a churn risk surge (312 members >70%), digital engagement decay (1,243 dormant), and military retiree NPS decline (214 members, -13 pts).

[Key Recommendations]
[1] Personalized rate match for military retirees — $580K investment to retain $31.6M in CD balances (ROI: 54:1)
[2] Multi-channel re-engagement for digital-dormant — upgrade from email-only to SMS + phone to 3x response rate
[3] Cross-sell acceleration for single-product holders — 487 members with 2.1x churn risk reduction potential

[Budget Request]
— Immediate: $240K for targeted outreach across all segments
— Q2 allocation: $580K if rate concession approved for military retirees

Want me to format this as a slide deck or email for distribution?

ret_which_segments_driving.ai_message:
The churn surge is being driven by three segments converging:

[1] Military Retirees (214 members) — the trigger
CD rate changes in Q1 pushed this segment from 58% to 71% avg churn risk. They're the most vocal (NPS -13 pts) and hold the highest individual LTV ($172K).

[2] Single-Product Holders (487 members) — the accelerant
Rate comparison shopping + low engagement creates a compounding effect. These members have no product stickiness — one bad experience and they leave.

[3] First-Time Applicants (312 members) — the friction fallout
Mortgage application friction didn't just cause drop-offs — it created a churn signal. 64% of stalled applicants now show elevated churn risk across all products.

The overlap matters: 47 military retirees are also showing engagement decay, meaning they're being hit by two churn drivers simultaneously.
suggested_chips: Which segments are most at risk?
